title: andropogon schoenanthus subsp. clandestinus
intro: Andropogon schoenanthus subsp. clandestinus, also known as Sand Paper Grass or Wild Cat's Whisker, is a type of grass native to Africa, characterized by its distinctive, hair-like leaves. Traditionally used in folk medicine, Andropogon schoenanthus subsp. clandestinus has been reported to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, treating wounds, and reducing fever, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In traditional herbal medicine, Andropogon schoenanthus subsp. clandestinus is often prepared as a tea or infusion by boiling its leaves in water, although the optimal methods and dosages for its use remain unclear.
